prerequisites: Physical console access to the device (serial or USB console cable)
Ability to power-cycle the device (for ROMMON-based recovery)
Know the device platform and OS to select the correct procedure
Authorization to perform password recovery (security policy may require approval)
procedure_name: Password Recovery and AAA Lockout Recovery (nxos)
purpose: Recover access to a device when admin credentials are lost or AAA server is unreachable [nxos — method: Admin password reset via loader/POAP; platforms: Nexus 9000, Nexus 3000]

rollback.action: No rollback needed — password reset is additive
If config was erased: restore from backup with 'copy scp://server/backup.cfg running-config'

steps.action: Connect console cable and power-cycle the device
steps.notes: You must be on the console port. SSH/telnet will not work if you're locked out.
Press Ctrl+C or Ctrl+] during boot when you see 'Press Ctrl-C or Ctrl-] to enter the loader prompt'. Timing is critical — watch for the prompt.
NX-OS 9.2+ on Nexus 9000. This boots the system with admin password reset to default. On older NX-OS, use 'boot nxos bootflash:<image> admin-password' instead.
After setting recoverpw, boot the image normally. The admin password will be reset.

steps.action: Log in with default credentials and reset password

commands.command:
configure terminal
username admin password <new_password> role network-admin

steps.notes: Log in as admin with no password (or default password depending on NX-OS version). Immediately set a new admin password.
Save the new password to startup-config. Verify with 'show running-config | include username admin'.

verification.look_for: Admin user logged in via console

warnings: Password recovery requires PHYSICAL console access — there is no remote recovery method
Some organizations disable password recovery for security (NX-OS: 'system no password-recovery', IOS-XE: 'no service password-recovery')
If password recovery is disabled, the ONLY option is to erase the configuration and start fresh
On IOS-XE: confreg 0x2142 skips startup-config on boot — do NOT forget to set it back to 0x2102
Document new credentials securely after recovery
If 'system no password-recovery' is configured, the only option is to wipe the config entirely
Nexus 7000 uses a different procedure — boot to kickstart image, then use 'admin-password' option
POAP (PowerOn Auto Provisioning) may trigger if config is erased — disable DHCP on mgmt before recovery
